takeaways: Janus Living prices upsized IPO at $840M — first new senior housing REIT to go public in years, trading on NYSE as JAN.
Fed holds at 3.50–3.75% with only one cut projected for all of 2026; oil spike from Strait of Hormuz disruption revives stagflation concerns.
44% of real estate investment committees distrust AI-generated analysis per Commercial Observer — trust, not capability, is now the primary barrier to AI adoption in CRE.

items.headline: Janus Living Prices Upsized IPO at $840M — New Senior Housing REIT Begins Trading on NYSE as "JAN"
items.summary: Senior housing REIT Janus Living, carved out of Healthpeak Properties, priced its upsized IPO at $20 per share, raising $840M — above its initial $740M target. It is the first new senior housing-focused public REIT listing in several years, reflecting renewed investor confidence in the sector.

items.headline: JLL: Senior Housing Transaction Volume Reaches Decade-High $24B — 86% of Investors Seeking More Exposure
items.summary: JLL's rolling four-quarter transaction volume for seniors housing reached $24B by year-end 2025 — the strongest pace in over a decade. 86% of surveyed investors are seeking to increase seniors housing exposure in 2026, up 10 percentage points year-over-year.

items.headline: Fortress Investment Group Launches 1031 Exchange Platform with 40% Senior Housing Allocation, Targeting $100–200M
items.summary: Fortress Investment Group announced a new 1031 exchange platform allocating ~40% of assets to senior living, targeting $100–200M in deployments through 2026. The structure allows investors to defer capital gains taxes by rolling proceeds into diversified CRE assets including senior housing.

items.headline: Fed Holds at 3.50–3.75%; Oil Spike From Strait of Hormuz Disruption Revives Stagflation Fears
items.summary: The FOMC voted on March 18 to hold rates at 3.50–3.75%, with one dissent favoring a cut. SOFR is tracking at ~3.67–3.71% for March and conventional commercial mortgage rates opened the week at 4.99%–5.19%. An oil price surge from Strait of Hormuz disruptions has revived stagflation concerns.

items.headline: Commercial Observer: 44% of Real Estate Investment Committees Distrust AI-Generated Analysis
items.summary: A Commercial Observer report found that 44% of investment committees distrust AI-generated analysis, and only 27% express confidence in AI for financial underwriting. The challenge is no longer capability — AI can parse 200+ lease variables in minutes — but governance, auditability, and explainability of outputs.

items.headline: VC Pours $16.7B Into Proptech in 2025 — Up 68% — But Rapid AI Advances Unsettling the CRE Tech Stack
items.summary: Proptech investment hit $16.7B in 2025, a 68% jump year-over-year, with AI-native platforms capturing ~$4.5B. Bisnow reports the pace of AI advancement is causing VCs to regroup, with concern that platforms built on 2024-era models are already being disrupted by newer agentic alternatives.

items.headline: Gartner: AI on Track to Eliminate Half of Middle Management "Coordination" Roles by End of 2026
items.summary: Gartner projects organizations actively deploying AI will eliminate ~50% of middle management roles by end of 2026. DDI's 2026 Leadership Trends report adds that 71% of leaders report increased stress and 77% of CHROs lack confidence in their leadership bench strength.

items.headline: Miami Premier Padel P1 Opens Today — World Elite Compete March 23–29 at Miami Beach Convention Center
items.summary: The Qatar Airways Premier Padel P1 kicks off today at the Miami Beach Convention Center, running through March 29 — the second consecutive year Miami hosts a P1-level stop. The PGA Tour Signature Event also returns to Trump Doral in 2026, projected to generate $100–200M in direct economic activity.
